title: Optimized protocol for DNA/RNA co-extraction from adults of Dirofilaria immitis
author: Lucchetti, Genchi, et al.
materials: Following ownersвЂ™ consent, 12 adult worms of D. immitis, respectively 6 females and 6 males, were collected during minimally-invasive surgical heartworm removal from two dogs previously diagnosed with patent D. immitis infection. Extracted worms were washed in HankвЂ™s Balanced Salt Solution medium (Sigma-AldrichВ®, Missouri, USA) at room temperature, checked for viability, counted and sexed. Three female and three male worms were individually placed into 15вЂ‰mL Falcon tubes and stored at в€’80вЂ‰В°C. Single worms were used as starting material for the RNA/DNA co-extraction.

Therefore, a solution of 150вЂ‰ng/ОјL of RNA in a final volume of 50вЂ‰ОјL was prepared for each sample. To each sample, 5вЂ‰ОјL of TURBOв„ў DNase buffer and 2вЂ‰ОјL of TURBOв„ў DNase (Thermo Fisher Scientific, Waltham, USA) were added, followed by incubation at 37вЂ‰В°C for 30вЂ‰min. After the first incubation, a further 2вЂ‰ОјL of TURBOв„ў DNase enzyme was added and incubated again at 37вЂ‰В°C for other 30вЂ‰min.

To obtain a fully pure RNA sample to be used for gene expression analysis, an additional purification step was performed. RNA samples were treated with AccuRT Genomic DNA Removal Kit (Abm, Richmond, Canada) according to the manufacturer protocol. Starting with 300вЂ‰ng of final RNA, and followed by an incubation at room temperature of 8вЂ‰min. For cDNA synthesis, the OneScriptВ® cDNA Synthesis Kit (Abm, Richmond, Canada) was used. The presence of any genomic contamination was checked by PCR reaction using GOTAQ Flexi DNA Polymerase Green G2 (Fisher MB, Rome, Italy) and 0.35 ОјM of specific primers designed on Dim-pgp-10 for cDNA (DimmScaf48-cDNA-F8: 5вЂІ-GCCATCGTAGGTCCATCAGGTTCTGGT-3вЂІ; DimmScaf48-cDNA-R12: 5вЂІ-TGTTCAACTGAAACGACCACACGTC-3вЂІ) (Sigma-AldrichВ®, Missouri, USA) [2]. The amplification protocol was characterized by a denaturation step at 95вЂ‰В°C for 1,30вЂ‰min, followed by 40 repeated cycles: 95вЂ‰В°C for 15вЂ‰s; 59.4вЂ‰В°C for 1вЂ‰min with a final elongation of 72вЂ‰В°C for 5вЂ‰min. Amplicons were run on a 2% agarose gel. For pure cDNA only one band would be of 155bp while in presence of genomic DNA contamination a second band of amplification would be visible at 476bp.

To validate the efficiency of proposed protocol, RNA products obtained with the just described protocol were compared to those obtained with the use of a product specific for the optimization and simplification of RNA isolation: Phasemakerв„ў Tubes (Thermo Fisher Scientific, Waltham, USA).

steps.title: DNA and RNA co-extraction
steps.instruction: To each tube containing 500вЂ‰ОјL of homogenized sample a further 500вЂ‰ОјL of TRIzolВ® reagent were added. They were treated according the TRIzolВ® Reagent manufacturer protocols [1]. Once the three phases were separated and the aqueous phase set apart from the other two, the two isolation steps were performed once again according to the manufacturer protocol, although a few changes were made at the elution step for both RNA and DNA procedures.

steps.title: DNA and RNA co-extraction
steps.instruction: The final elution of RNA samples was performed in 100вЂ‰ОјL of DEPC water, and the rehydration of the sample was performed at 56вЂ‰В°C per 13вЂ‰min. Some samples, due to the presence of a greater pellet, were placed in a water bath at 56вЂ‰В°C for a longer time, until the pellet was fully rehydrated.

steps.title: DNA and RNA co-extraction
steps.instruction: The final elution of DNA samples was performed in 300вЂ‰ОјL of DEPC water, and they were then placed in a water bath at 46вЂ‰В°C for 15вЂ‰min.

steps.title: DNA and RNA co-extraction
steps.instruction: After resuspension, all samples were briefly vortexed and analyzed at the spectrophotometer (BioSpectrometerВ® fluorescence with ОјCuvettaВ® G 1.0, Eppendorf, Hamburg, Germany). For each sample, data concerning concentration (ng/ОјL) and quality, described as 260/280 and 260/230 values were collected.

steps.title: RNA purification for down-stream molecular applications
steps.instruction: In order to guarantee RNA quality which would allow down-stream analysis, such as quantitative/relative RT-PCR, each sample required the elimination of any possible residue of genomic DNA.

steps.title: RNA purification for down-stream molecular applications
steps.instruction: A 500вЂ‰ОјL of TRIzolВ® Reagent was added to each tube and a second RNA extraction was performed following the manufacturer protocol. The final elution was performed in 35вЂ‰ОјL of DEPC water and as performed for the first RNA extraction, pellets were rehydrated at 56вЂ‰В°C for 13вЂ‰min. All samples were quantified at the spectrophotometer. Results obtained from both the first and the second RNA extraction as well as those obtained for DNA have been reported in Table1.Table 1Data collection reporting concentrations (ng/ОјL) as well as the 260/280 and 260/230 parameters measure by spectrophotometer analysis of three biological replicates per each sex. All samples were analyzed individually and per each of the six worms the mean values of the technical replicates were calculated and listed in the table. steps.title: RNA extraction through Phasemakerв„ў Tubes
steps.instruction: Three adult worms of D. immitis per each sex were analyzed. Thus, two samples per each female and one per each male were used for the extraction procedure with Phasemakerв„ў Tubes. The manufacturerвЂ™s protocol was followed. All remaining samples, respectively two samples per each female and one per each male, were processed using the proposed co-extraction protocol. Results from both RNA extractions are reported and compared in Table 2.Table 2Comparison between concentration and quality of samples from untreated worms (Control) extracted following either the newly developed protocol or using the Phasemakerв„ў Tubes manufacturer protocol. substeps.instruction: Three adult worms of D. immitis per each sex were analyzed. Thus, two samples per each female and one per each male were used for the extraction procedure with Phasemakerв„ў Tubes. The manufacturerвЂ™s protocol was followed. All remaining samples, respectively two samples per each female and one per each male, were processed using the proposed co-extraction protocol. Results from both RNA extractions are reported and compared in Table 2.Table 2Comparison between concentration and quality of samples from untreated worms (Control) extracted following either the newly developed protocol or using the Phasemakerв„ў Tubes manufacturer protocol. steps.title: RNA extraction through Phasemakerв„ў Tubes
steps.instruction: When compared, results obtain from the Phasemakerв„ў Tubes with those obtained following the co-extraction protocol; both their quality and quantity of RNA appeared similar between the protocols. In fact, no statistical difference was observed between the two different extraction procedures when the single worms were considered separately. On the other hand, when the overall results from each sex were compared, the statistical analysis showed a marked significant difference (p-valueвЂ‰=вЂ‰0.005) in female step 1 RNA and a less significant difference for final RNA (p-valueвЂ‰=вЂ‰0.03). No difference was observed for all RNA extracted from males. This difference is probably related to the difference in size among the female worms used for this assay.

steps.title: RNA extraction through Phasemakerв„ў Tubes
steps.instruction: Results obtained validate the possibility of using the proposed protocol as an efficient option for simultaneous extraction of good quality of both DNA and RNA from a unique sample of adult D. immitis. To the authorsвЂ™ knowledge, this is the first attempt to develop a protocol for DNA/RNA co-extraction in nematodes.

description: A method for co-extraction of DNA and RNA from a single worm of D. immitis has been validated using TRIzolВ® as the main reagent. The described extraction procedure grants maximum exploitation of a single sample of adult D. immitis worm, allowing the possibility of focusing on different levels, either on the parasite itself or on its symbiont, and thus investigating different aspects of their metabolisms.
